stall_name: Hong Heng Fried Sotong Prawn Mee (Tiong Bahru Market)
reviews: Many people would know about this stall because of their recognition on Michelin Guide’s Bib Gourmand for a few years. The stall opens around 10:30am and I would already see a queue in front over the weekend. The hawker cooks it batches by batches, so the queue is actually moved pretty fast once the he finished cooking it. His version of fried Hokkien mee here is definitely more on the wet version, which some may find it lack of wok hey taste. But I like it as he cooked the noodle with seafood broth. You must squeeze the lime and mix all of them with chili for a better flavour.

Prawn Mee. Feature the medium size, the Hokkien mee is wet version. Cooked in seafood broth, quite tasty, the ingredients fresh, wish have more wok hei. 💰$5 for medium. 📍 Hong Heng Fried Sotong Prawn Mee. Tiong Bahru Market , #02-01. Singapore 168898,

Serving since the 1980s and having attained the Michelin Bib Gourmand award over the last 7 consecutive years, 3rd-generation owner Manfred Lim carries on much like before. . Their rendition tends towards balanced, leaning more to 'wet'. A robust prawn and pork stock infuses the large portion of noodles, gooey soft chewy (almost al-dente) with grainy savoury salty flavour, but has less wok hei / breath of the wok. . Garnished with soft chicken egg, crisp bean sprouts, springy squid, bouncy fish cake, and crunchy deshelled prawns, combining for a vegetal eggy sweet savoury salty flavour. Finished with lime juice for a zesty sour tangy touch.

contact_and_links.address: 30 Seng Poh Road #02-01 Tiong Bahru Market Singapore 168898

google.google_name: Hong Heng Fried Sotong Prawn Mee 鸿興炒蘇東蝦麵

reviews.author: Wei
reviews.text: tried the Hokkien mee after seeing the long queue. Despite the crowd, service was fast and I waited only about 10 minutes. The hawker and his daughter were friendly and efficient. The noodles had decent wok hei, though the flavour was a bit on the mild side and definitely needed chilli to lift it. Ingredients were fresh and well cooked. Overall, a solid and enjoyable plate, especially if you like a lighter taste profile.

reviews.author: Damien Wong
reviews.text:
Added another Michelin hawker stall to my list.
The queue moved surprisingly fast despite the weekday lunch crowd—there were about 10 people ahead of me, but I only waited 15 minutes.
I was a little disappointed, though. The prawn flavour wasn’t as intense as I’d hoped; the noodles were quite wet and lacked noticeable wok hei or a strong prawn taste. That said, the $7 portion was very generous, with around 6 prawns.

The chilli wasn’t particularly impressive either.

All of this is just my personal preference—I’m not criticising the stall at all. Please go try it for yourself. I’ve simply had non-Michelin Hokkien mee that I enjoyed more.

reviews.author: Teck Chong Law
reviews.text:
It's been many years since my last visit to this stall, before they first received the Michelin guide recognition in 2016.

There was a long queue, around 15-18 pax ahead of me during lunch time.  Luckily there was no one ordering many plates/packets, the queue cleared within 15-20 minutes.

When I saw the slice fish cake on display, I already quite disappointed.

I ordered the $7 portion and it came with five prawns (one prawn hidden below and only found it when I was half way eating).

Happy with the large serving portion and five medium size prawns.

Disappointed with the taste.  No HKM taste.  It is like an average normal fried noodles.  The chilli saved me.

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text: No frills fried sotong prawn mee that draws a queue. The line moves steadily as the chef cooks and serves up plate after plate of al dente yellow noodles and thick bee hoon, mixed with fresh prawns and slices of squid and fish cake. There are three sizes to choose from ($5, $6, $7) and the stall accepts cash payment only. The chili adds a layer of spice and umami to the dish that won’t numb your taste buds. Squeeze a splash of calamansi juice over the noodles for a zesty lift to this classic local noodle dish.

reviews.author: CK C
reviews.text: Noodles lacked wok hei and tasted rather bland, and required the chilli to provide the flavours. Do note that there is no pork in the $5 plate. Otherwise, the amount of ingredients were rather generous. Cash only.

stall_name: Fried Kway Teow • Fried Oyster (Tiong Bahru Market)
reviews: Aw, Shucks. This being on the ‘Cheerless Chow’ list might be harsh, but it wasn’t good enough to eat...
Orh Jian ($4) Read about this stall in Burpple and decided to try it because I’m all about unhealthy...
For Wok Hei-Laden Char Kway Teow A hidden gem in Tiong Bahru, this stall is your best bet if you li...

contact_and_links.address: 30 Seng Poh Road #02-11 Tiong Bahru Market Singapore 168898

reviews.author: Sandra
reviews.text: I should have read the reviews beforehand…was misled by the old stall and the old stallholders cooking at the pan as well as the long queue. Decided to give this stall a try and joined the long queue. Boy were we disappointed! Dry, oily and pretty much tasteless with zero wok hei! Decent prices at $4/$5 a plate. Cash only…in this time and age, they should consider cashless payments. Perhaps being the only Char Kway Teow stall in this market has allowed the business to thrive. Not worth another try!

reviews.author: Thomas Yong
reviews.text:
Service to take order very bad. Do not accept cash.
Aunty said cannot remember & act arrogant.
No service if we want to take this stall. First time, eating & will consider for return

reviews.author: Siah H
reviews.text:
First time trying. Wok Hei is present. I wld say its tasty. Service is just average.

Order the $4 portion... comes complete with chinese sausages and blood clams, reasonable portion i wld say.

This stall only accepts Cash, no paynow or cdc.

Located at level 2 of Tiong Bahru Market.

reviews.author: Jonathan Tan
reviews.text:
Not worth the hype. Tried the Tiong Bahru Fried Kway Teow stall and was left thoroughly disappointed. Despite the long queue, the food turned out to be average at best. The waiting time was unnecessarily long. Not because of a crowd, but because the chef seemed to be taking his own sweet time. They clearly can’t cope with the lunch crowd efficiently.

What’s worse, they still accept only cash, even after the hawker centre’s recent renovation. In this day and age, people are using PayNow, CDC vouchers, and other cashless options. It's ridiculous to cling onto old ways, especially when the rest of the hawker centre has moved forward.

Yes, I’m aware this stall used to be popular and might’ve been featured in newspapers or food blogs but that was the past. The quality no longer matches the reputation. Don’t rely on your old fame when you’re no longer keeping up with the times or delivering on taste.

Would not recommend. Plenty of better options around.

reviews.author: LUFFY Gaming Streamer
reviews.text: Was rushing for work. Having a Take away. After ordering. Notice that they cook the food plate by plate. Waited for like 30min knowing that it my turn. The owner given the food to someone after me. After telling them I’m infront of him. Rather than apologize. They tell me even I’m first I still have to wait? What kind of explanation is that. Worst experience the food taste plain. N no wok hei. Not worth trying!!

stall_name: Tiong Bahru Bak Chor Mee (Tiong Bahru Market)
reviews: Teochew meatball soup 4.5nett. I don't think this was as good as I rmbed...
6nett portion. Still the textbook version it was. It's blasphemous there's no queue here...
Bcm 6nett portion. Teochew meatball was incredibly good, probably homemade...

contact_and_links.address: 30 Seng Poh Road #02-01 Tiong Bahru Market Singapore 168898

reviews.author: LYSL28
reviews.text: This is my to go stall for Bak Chor noodles. Kelvin is very kind & meticulously take care of his elderly customer. Witnessed him asking a helper if there’s a need for him to cut the noodle since it’s for her ah mah. Also, he asked an elderly uncle to take a sit 1st, he will serve him instead of standing in the ‘Q’. My pics taken/shown here was after mixing all the awesome, right amount of condiments, mee pok is always to the right texture to my liking. I will also pack the frozen meat balls when there’s steamboat back home.

reviews.author: Warren Kok
reviews.text:
I can’t remember if J have eaten here before

Noodle texture is the wider one for mee gia
Liver and meat is decent
Taste is ok

Overall rating 7.8/10

Owner is friendly

reviews.author: Jun Wee
reviews.text:
Kelvin, the store owner is serious about what he does and makes decent meatballs with juicy mince meat inside.

For S$4 a bowl, the meal is decent and has a generous amount of minced and slice pork with liver.

The soup is delicious. Kelvin prepares a secret soup base with rather complex, multi-dimensional flavour.

reviews.author: Kai
reviews.text:
stall owner is very friendly and busy cooking, taking orders, receiving payment

good meatballs

reviews.author: Sean Goh
reviews.text:
Happen to be running some appointment in Tiong Bahru in the morning, decided to drop by this hawker centre for some breakfast. Have a particular stall in mind that I wanted to try but they not operating that day. Walked around and chanced upon this stall selling minced meat noodle. This is my first time patronizing this stall. Apart from minced meat noodle, they also sell fishball noodle as well as this sliced abalone noodle.

Decided to go with the minced meat noodle which starts from SGD$4 onwards. The bowl of minced meat noodle comes with minced and sliced pork, liver, fishball with filling and sliced braised mushroom. Noodle was way overcooked, as a result the noodle was soggy. Sauce was also too diluted because the noodle wasn’t drained well. All the ingredient was fine except the liver, which could be cooked a little longer. Liver has a very strong gamey smell.

Overall, pretty bad experience. I didn’t even finish my bowl of noodle.

stall_name: Tiong Bahru Mian Jian Kueh (Tiong Bahru Market)

contact_and_links.address: 30 Seng Poh Road #02-34 Tiong Bahru Market Singapore 168898

reviews.author: Touristmouse
reviews.text:
Update Oct 25: Came back for the Brown Rice Peanut $1.10 and Round Red Bean $1.10 and enjoyed both of them. The texture is so soft and chewy. I like that the kueh here are small than typical ones  so you can try more flavours.

Sep 2025: I was just planning to get coffee at the next stall when I came across this three-in-one mee jiang kueh for $1.40 and was tickled by the combination. I've never seen anything like it before. It's perfect for days when you just can't decide, and the combo really makes sense.

I got one to try, and it was still warm, with a soft and fluffy skin. The filling was generous, and somehow, the mixture really worked. You should give them a try if you're in the area. I'm glad I got this, as the coffee was just meh.

reviews.author: CG
reviews.text: Excellent brown rice peanut butter min jiang kueh ($1.20). I was lucky to get one fresh off the pan and piping hot. It wasn't overly sweet or too heavy on the peanut butter, avoiding that overly rich feeling. The edges were ultra crispy. Probably the best peanut butter min jiang kueh I've had, and without too much guilt. The size is a bit small, but you're paying for quality. Makes for a great snack.

reviews.author: Cheong BJ
reviews.text:
On 03/08 @3.18pm I went to your outlet @ Tiong Bahru Plaza.
I ordered 2pcs of dianasaur munchi and was made to wait 30mins for the munchi.
When I gotten my munchi, I am sorry to say it wasn't as I expected.
The filling was not spread evenly within the munchi and they were given to me as cold ( the pancake were not heated ).  I have had your munchi frm other outlets but they were all nice and warm with filling spreaded evenly.
I was very disappointed despite my wait I got 2 cold munchi.  I will not be going back to your Tiong Bahru Plaza outlet anymore.

reviews.author: Brett DiNovi (Personal)
reviews.text:
So good. This was our morning sweet treat throughout our time in Singapore.

Morning walk to the hawker center where we'd grab something savory at another stand and follow it up with these pancake bites.

For us, the small pucks were the preferred texture and coconut (orange color) was the preferred flavor because it provided some sweetness to balance the taste.

We tried basically everything during our week in town but kept coming back to that combination.

From there it'd probably be: red bean, sesame and peanut in that order.

Definitely recommended as a new traveler to the area.

reviews.author: K
reviews.text:
Served hot, the texture of the skin was perfectly spongey and chewy at the same time, and the filling - both red bean and peanut - was chef's kiss! Not too sweet, unlike some stores where you could actually chew the gritty texture of granulated sugar. There wasn't too little filling, but neither was it a lot.

HOWEVER my biggest qualm is that its extremely thin. The width is about the width of two fingers... this for a $1?? Personally if the size was just a bit more generous like the good old days, it would've been 5 stars!

You can decide if size matters or not lol but the texture + fragrance is definitely worth a shot!
